title: URDF Kinematic-Camera World Model
description: Research track: compile explicit robot morphology, time-aligned action/state, and calibrated third-person/wrist-camera trajectories into multi-view, layer-aware controls for generative robot rollouts and policy evaluation.
summary: The robotics analogue of an AI-film previs workflow is not raw URDF prompting. It is a deterministic 3D reference compiler: import the robot URDF, drive it with commanded/measured joint trajectories, re-project the same world-frame motion through calibrated third-person and wrist cameras, and condition a pluggable video generator with synchronized spatial controls.
details: Research thesis: separate controllable robot geometry and camera motion from learned environment appearance/reaction. URDF/FK anchors the articulated body; the video model predicts scene, object, contact, deformation, and residual appearance dynamics around that anchor.

Film-workflow mapping: scene blocking becomes URDF/scene assets; Blender animation becomes timestamped q_cmd/q_meas/q_sim and gripper/object state; phone cinematography becomes fixed eye-to-hand or dynamic eye-in-hand camera TF plus intrinsics/extrinsics; Seedance becomes a replaceable multimodal video backend.

Compiler contract: URDF meshes/joints/limits + action/state provenance + camera calibration/TF -> per-view robot render, skeleton, silhouette/link-ID, depth, normals, pointmap/flow, ray maps, visibility and robot/object/contact/background layer manifests. Raw URDF remains provenance and validation metadata rather than the primary learned token stream.

Novelty boundary: BridgeV2W already renders URDF/camera-conditioned embodiment masks; Kinema4D already projects URDF-driven 4D trajectories into pointmaps; OSCAR already uses cross-embodiment 2D skeleton controls. URDF + action + camera alone is therefore not a defensible new claim.

Candidate claim: jointly model held-out URDF morphology, explicit commanded/measured action provenance, calibrated dynamic wrist plus third-person cameras, action/camera factorization, and layer-aware environmental reaction in one multi-view world-model interface.

Evaluation principle: use morphology x action x camera factorial splits and compare text, sim RGB, skeleton, mask, pointmap and full 3D/layer bundles. Judge action/camera adherence, cross-view geometry, contact/scene reaction, long-rollout persistence, and executable policy-evaluation correlation rather than FVD alone.

Promotion decision 2026-07-11: move from Survey to Research because the direction now has a concrete compiler interface, direct baselines, falsifiable ablations, and an executable evaluation path. The Xiaohongshu/Film Space workflow is inspiration for the interface, not evidence of robotics novelty.

references.title: Film Space: iPhone ARKit virtual-film camera workflow
references.notes: Workflow inspiration and implicit reference-video baseline. The current public code tracks camera-to-world transforms internally but records rendered video to Photos; it does not expose a documented per-frame trajectory export or Blender bridge.

references.title: Xiaohongshu reference: AI film previs and camera workflow
references.notes: Shared workflow inspiration: generated scene -> 3D blocking -> camera path -> reference-conditioned video generation. Treat as creative-process context, not technical evidence.

references.title: Seedance 2.0 Official Launch
references.notes: Officially documented multimodal reference backend that can consume images/video/audio and reference composition, motion and camera movement. Seedance 2.5 remains unverified and is not a project dependency.

references.title: BridgeV2W: URDF- and camera-rendered embodiment masks
references.notes: Closest direct baseline: coordinate-space actions are converted into pixel-aligned masks rendered from URDF and camera parameters, then injected through a ControlNet-style pathway.

references.title: Kinema4D: URDF-driven 4D trajectories and pointmap control
references.notes: Direct baseline for precise kinematic control, pointmap conditioning, synchronized RGB/geometry generation and cross-embodiment claims.

references.title: OSCAR: Omni-Embodiment Action-Conditioned World Model
references.notes: Strong skeleton-only baseline: renders 2D kinematic skeletons as a unified condition across robot arms and human hands for video generation and policy evaluation.

references.title: EnerVerse-AC: action conditioning with dynamic multi-view ray maps
references.notes: Robot world-model baseline for multi-level action conditioning, dynamic multi-view ray maps, failure trajectories and policy evaluation.

references.title: RynnWorld-Teleop: depth-aware action-conditioned digital teleoperation
references.notes: Relevant Image Layered Policy reference: depth-aware skeleton controls, human-to-robot curriculum and causal streaming generation show the value of explicit geometric control without requiring a full URDF interface.

references.title: WorldCraft: camera-invariant world trajectories
references.notes: Method reference for separating object motion from camera-induced image displacement by storing trajectories in world coordinates and re-projecting under the current camera pose.

references.title: SymphoMotion: joint camera and object-motion control
references.notes: Non-robotics but methodologically direct baseline for explicit camera trajectories plus 3D object trajectories in one video-generation system.

references.title: MoveIt URDF/SRDF tutorial
references.notes: Reference for URDF link/joint geometry, joint limits and root/virtual-joint semantics required by the compiler contract.

risks_decisions: Novelty risk: BridgeV2W, Kinema4D and OSCAR already cover major parts of URDF/kinematic projection. The paper cannot claim novelty from importing URDF, rendering masks/skeletons, or adding camera parameters alone.

Morphology risk: real URDF assets often omit or mismatch visual meshes, materials, scale, mimic/passive joints, closed chains and collision/inertial detail. Record asset fidelity and reject unsupported topology instead of silently rendering the wrong body.

Action risk: commanded, simulated and measured state are not interchangeable. Preserve absolute/delta, joint/Cartesian, position/velocity/torque, control rate, execution latency and timestamps so the model is not trained against the wrong motion.

Camera risk: third-person extrinsics may be fixed, but an eye-in-hand camera has time-varying T_world_camera derived from FK and T_link_camera. Distortion, cropping, clock alignment, exposure and rolling shutter can dominate fast wrist-motion error.

Physics risk: robot trajectory does not determine object response uniquely. Object pose/deformation, contact, force/tactile state and scene geometry need separate sidecars or uncertainty gating; otherwise the generator can satisfy robot geometry while hallucinating task success.

Backend risk: Seedance is useful for a quick reference-video probe but is closed and currently documented publicly as Seedance 2.0. Keep the compiler/backbone boundary explicit and maintain an open reproducible fallback.

Film Space boundary: use its staging/camera workflow as an analogy and implicit-reference baseline. Do not describe its current public implementation as a calibrated TF exporter or Blender integration.

Research decision 2026-07-11: promote this card to Research and position the contribution around joint morphology-action-camera factorization, held-out embodiment evaluation, layer-aware scene reaction and executable policy evaluation.
asset_alt: URDF kinematic-camera world model pipeline from robot morphology, time-aligned action/state, and calibrated multi-camera trajectories into per-view spatial controls and layer-aware video generation.
asset_caption: Research pipeline: compile URDF/FK, commanded or measured motion, and fixed/dynamic camera calibration into synchronized multi-view controls; let a pluggable video world model generate environment reactions while the robot geometry stays explicitly anchored.
